changelog: Fix Overview/Device-Monitor deep link bouncing to Device list: the back-gesture interceptor on OverviewPage and DeviceMonitorPage treated every popstate event as a real back-swipe and immediately redirected to /devices — but popstate can also fire from a non-back-gesture navigation into these pages (e.g. a deep link from a push notification, or any hard hash navigation rather than in-app router.navigate()), so landing straight on /device/:id/dashboard could bounce right back to the device list. The interceptor now only redirects if the address is still the guarded page's own URL when popstate fires (i.e. the popped entry really is our own placeholder), otherwise it lets the router render the new route normally.

Fix Sleep Mode not writing AC charge power (0x0085): the scheduler only sent at time-window edges and its enforce effect had empty deps, so toggling Sleep Mode on/off or editing the sleep window sent nothing until a boundary crossing happened to occur while the Device Info page was open — you'd see no 0x85 write. Now the power is applied immediately on enable and whenever sleepFrom/sleepTo change (sleep phase -> Sierro 1000 150W / Sierro 2000 300W; wake phase -> 400W / 800W), turning Sleep Mode off restores the wake power, and the boundary enforcement still runs. Locked the per-model values with a unit test (getPowers)

Fix Android 'cannot get Bluetooth permission': on native the provisioning screen initialized bleStatus to 'ready' and never routed to the actionable Permission Required / Bluetooth Off screens, so when the BLE (Nearby devices) permission was denied the user only saw a transient error toast with no way to fix it. Now a scan failure is classified: permission/denied -> Permission Required screen with Open Settings + a 'try again' button and Android 12+ 'Nearby devices' wording; adapter off/unavailable -> Bluetooth Off screen. Note: BLE runtime permission/scan is now exercisable on-device for the first time; if the OS prompt still doesn't appear, grant Nearby devices via Settings using the new button

Fix Android/iOS login timeout: enable CapacitorHttp so native fetch/XHR go through the native HTTP stack instead of the WebView. In the WebView the app's origin is https://localhost, which the API's CORS allowlist rejects, and the Origin/Referer headers apiClient sets are dropped as forbidden headers — so login (and every API call) hung until the 10s timeout. Native HTTP bypasses CORS and actually sends those headers, matching what the backend expects. Web is unaffected (CapacitorHttp is native-only). Requires a rebuilt app; the next Android APK from CI includes the fix

Push cropped for first release (P0-4): new PUSH_ENABLED flag (src/config/webPush.ts, default false) because remote push cannot work yet (no APNs entitlement, no google-services.json, backend token endpoints unimplemented). While off: the Settings 'Push Notifications' section is hidden (tree-shaken from the production bundle), no push init on login (App.tsx), and the onboarding permission gate no longer requests notification permission (PermissionsGate). This removes the store-rejection risk of asking for notification permission for a feature that can't deliver. Flip VITE_PUSH_ENABLED=true once the backend + APNs/FCM credentials are ready to restore the full path (code retained). Updated docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md P0-4 status

Brand assets (P0-3): replace the Capacitor placeholder app icon and splash on BOTH platforms with the SIERRO wordmark (white background, black text, per brand). Generated the full set with sharp from assets/ sources (assets/gen-assets.mjs, reproducible): iOS AppIcon 1024 + Splash imageset 2732; Android launcher icons + round + adaptive foreground (wordmark scaled into the adaptive safe zone so circular/rounded masks don't clip it) across mdpi..xxxhdpi, and portrait/landscape splashes at every density. Adaptive icon background is white. Splash screen backgroundColor set to #FFFFFF to match. Note: a wide wordmark as a square icon reads small on phone home screens — an 'S' monogram would be larger; kept the full wordmark per request

Post-review fixes (7-dimension release audit). Two R1 realtime regressions fixed: (1) in fast-report mode an offline device kept showing 'Connected' with frozen readings because /state/latest returns the last cloud state forever — online status is now derived only from the cloud isOnline flag plus successful PASSTHROUGH round-trips; (2) a fast-report session could be orphaned (device stuck uploading at high rate) if the page was left mid-start — the active flag is now set before the start await so cleanup always stops it. Privacy honesty: removed false usage-analytics claims from the privacy policy and the no-op Anonymous Analytics toggle from Data Export; export stamps the real app version. Native readiness: Android versionName injected from src/version.json + versionCode from CI, allowBackup=false + data_extraction_rules; iOS PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy added. Release engineering: E2E CI now builds/serves and tests THIS commit (was testing production); real-account E2E creds moved to env and skipped when absent. Updated docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md with all 7 dimensions

Release hardening (RELEASE_PLAN M2). P0-2: 'Delete Account' now actually calls the deleteAccount API (POST /user/logout/account) with loading state and error handling, then clears the session — previously the reachable flow only logged out, which fails Apple 5.1.1(v) and Play's account-deletion policy. P0-5: added NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription/NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription to iOS Info.plist (the linked @capacitor/camera plugin references photo-library APIs -> avoids ITMS-90683 upload rejection). P1-5: Modbus Debug / Debug Params / BLE Debug pages and their Device-Info entry links are now hidden in release builds behind DEV_TOOLS_ENABLED (import.meta.env.DEV || VITE_ENABLE_DEV_TOOLS==='true'), so consumers can't reach raw-register/passthrough control surfaces. P1-6: set ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false in Info.plist (standard HTTPS/MD5 only) to stop the export-compliance prompt on every upload

Security: remove public/api-test.html — a debug console with a hardcoded AppSecret that was bundled into dist and published to GitHub Pages (publicly reachable). Also removed the local test_api.py containing a real account password. The AppSecret should be treated as leaked and rotated with the platform vendor. Added docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md: full App Store / Play Store readiness review and phased development plan (P0 blockers through P3 release engineering)

UI polish + iOS/Android adaptation (all 4 batches). Batch 1: transparent tap-highlight (no more gray flash on Android taps), overscroll-behavior locks page rubber-banding, touch-action manipulation on controls, user-select off on buttons/nav, Android windowBackground/statusBar/navigationBar unified to #141414 (kills launch white-flash), viewport allows pinch-zoom again (accessibility), tabular-nums extended to battery %% and Smart Schedule savings. Batch 2: new native UX bootstrap - StatusBar styled (light icons on dark), Android hardware back button handled (inner pages go back, root pages minimize the app instead of exiting), Keyboard resize=body + focused input scrolled into view; haptic feedback on toggles (light) and pull-to-refresh (medium) via @capacitor/haptics with web vibrate fallback. Batch 3: removed all 15 remaining transition-all (explicit properties only), Overview sections now stagger-enter at 100ms steps. Batch 4: iOS project generated (npx cap add ios) with Bluetooth/Camera/Local-Network usage descriptions in Info.plist, plus a macOS CI workflow building an unsigned simulator binary to keep the iOS project compiling. Android now registers 9 native plugins. Verified: typecheck clean, build ok, 25/25 local e2e passed

R1 realtime pipeline: new useLiveDeviceStatus hook is the single live-status entry for Overview. It probes platform fast-report support on entering the page; if supported it starts a fast-report session (device raises its cloud reporting rate) and polls the cheap /state/latest endpoint every 5s instead of occupying the Modbus passthrough channel; unsupported/old-firmware devices automatically fall back to the previous passthrough READ_ALL_STATUS polling. Fast report is stopped on leaving the page. Same safeguards as before: visibility-gated, no overlapping requests, live values cleared after 3 consecutive failures. Protocol decode verified with synthetic frames (good/corrupted-CRC/truncated + Int16 temp + 0x0133 state enum), typecheck clean, 25/25 local e2e passed

Engineering hardening (P1-P3 from architecture review). Typecheck: fixed invalid tsconfig ignoreDeprecations that made tsc unrunnable, fixed all 42 type errors (demoData drifted from DeviceListItem: ids/station/dtu ids are strings, state is a numeric enum, applyMode is a string; LucideIcon prop types; AppSettings.privacyAcknowledged; LoginData.email; PeakValleyEnableRequest.deviceId; PWA virtual module types; NotificationOptions.renotify), and build now runs tsc --noEmit first so type errors fail CI

API client: 5xx and timeouts now retry with backoff (previously only DNS/connection errors retried); HTTP 401 responses now go through the token-refresh flow too (previously only business-code 401/1001/1002 did)

Protocol layer: new decodePassthroughBase64() is the single entry for passthrough base64->registers decode (CRC-checked); OverviewPage live poll and deviceStore rated-params fetch both use it instead of duplicated inline decoding

Polling: Overview's 4 pollers (5s passthrough / 30s state / 60s alarms / 60s energy flow) now skip ticks while the page is hidden, and the 5s passthrough poll no longer issues overlapping requests when a call takes >5s
Fix native BLE scan leak: closing the provisioning sheet now stops the LE scan and clears the 10s auto-stop timer (destroyProvisionManager also stops scanning)
Fix QR Rescan frozen frame: the camera stream is re-acquired after a successful scan stopped it, so Rescan actually scans again
Realtime simulator now runs only in Demo (guest) mode - a real logged-in session no longer writes synthetic power history to IndexedDB
Cleanup: removed dead hooks (useDeviceWebSocket, useOfflineSync) and root-level log/capture artifacts (push_log*.txt, captured-*.json, test-login-real.mjs)
Signing credentials can now be injected at build time via VITE_IOT_APP_ID / VITE_IOT_APP_SECRET (localStorage override still wins; bundled fallback documented as public)

Data-integrity fixes (P0 from architecture review): 1) passthrough live decode now rejects frames with bad CRC (OverviewPage 5s poll + rated-params fetch) so corrupted responses can no longer show garbage SOC/power or mark the device online; 2) Overview live values (livePt) are cleared after 3 consecutive passthrough failures so a disconnected device falls back to cloud state instead of freezing on the last frame as 'Connected'; 3) fix System State Machine (0x0133) decode — it is a numeric enum, not a bitmask, so states like Charging(6) no longer display as Power-Up

Fix Wi-Fi Setup doing nothing when tapped: the Add-Device sheet's 'Wi-Fi Setup' option had no action (just closed the sheet). It now launches the BLE provisioning flow — scan nearby devices over Bluetooth (added or not), pick one, and set its Wi-Fi SSID/password. Also fixed handleBleScan dead-ending on the native app (navigator.bluetooth is absent in the WebView) by going straight to provisioning on native, and the device-scan screen now auto-starts the Bluetooth scan on entry
Email-code login: Obtain Code now requests the LOGIN captcha intent (CaptchaIntent.LOGIN '3'), not the register captcha, so the emailed code is the correct login verification code

Fix Android APK not requesting Bluetooth permission: the git-tracked Capacitor build files (capacitor.settings.gradle / capacitor.build.gradle) were stale and listed only 2 of 6 plugins, and capacitor.plugins.json was git-ignored — so a build that skipped `cap sync` shipped an APK with NO native BLE/camera/push/local-notification plugin. BleClient.initialize() then failed and the runtime permission prompt never appeared. Regenerated the build files (all 6 plugins) and now track capacitor.plugins.json so the native plugins are always compiled in and registered
Sleep Mode screen: translate remaining Chinese to English (AC 充电功率 -> AC charging power; 降至/恢复 -> arrows)

Overview: while on the page, poll live params via Modbus passthrough (READ_ALL_STATUS 0x0100) every 5s and update AC/Solar/Output/Battery/Temp (decodeLiveStatus); stops when leaving the page. Live values take priority over the 30s cloud state; a passthrough reply marks the device online
Battery Priority now drives PV/Battery Priority (0x86) via passthrough: Savings -> enable 0x01AA, Backup -> disable 0xAA01 (sent on Save alongside setWorkMode)

Sleep Mode now drives AC realtime charge power (0x0085) via passthrough: at sleep time -> Sierro 1000 150W / Sierro 2000 300W; at wake time -> 400W / 800W. Uses the device model profile (was writing rated 0x0024 and 1000W for 2000)

Passthrough protocol: add registers 0x54 (PV/Battery Priority Min SOC, %, default 30), 0x85 (AC realtime charge power, W, default 400), 0x86 (PV/Battery Priority on=0x01AA/off=0xAA01); new FRAMES + Passthrough page presets + REG_DESC parsing

Overview: add pull-to-refresh (fetches live AC input / Solar input / AC output + online state); 30s poll now also refreshes device details so isOnline stays fresh (params no longer appear stuck). Confirmed field mapping is correct: AC=exchangeChargingPower, Solar=generationPower, Output=outputPower
Fix: model picker sheet was rendered in the Device Settings screen, so tapping Model on Device Info did nothing until going back; moved the sheet into the Device Info screen where the Model row lives

Device Info: tap Model to manually select Sierro 1000/2000 — applies that model's default rated params (capacity/power/charge power/battery type/health) and serial prefix to the current device, for devices added before model selection existed

Add Device: choose model (Sierro 1000 / 2000) -> auto-generate serial number + default rated params (Rated Power, Rated Capacity, Rated Charging Power, Battery Type LFP, Battery Health 100%); saved per device and shown on Device Info (new Rated Charging Power row). BLE param sync now merges, preserving the model profile

Notifications: parse alarm code/key into readable text (alarmText dictionary + humanize fallback) so all history records show a description, not just Mains power failure; harden history filter so empty-key records are never hidden; same parsing in Overview alerts

WiFi setup: native app now scans and lists nearby BLE (SSL_) devices in-app (BleClient.requestLEScan); tap a device to connect and proceed to its Wi-Fi SSID/password config. Web keeps the OS Bluetooth picker. Fixed native being blocked by the navigator.bluetooth permission check

Device list power toggle now actually controls the device via Modbus passthrough: ON writes 0x0080<-0x01AA (AC_POWER_ON), OFF writes 0x0080<-0xAA01 (AC_POWER_OFF); optimistic UI with rollback + error on failure, disabled while in flight
Revert pure-black backgrounds back to #141414 (base token/body/manifest/native + page roots ink-13->ink-12); cards remain ink-10. Kept the e2e selector fix
Bottom nav: reduce bottom gap 8px -> 4px (sit closer to the bottom edge)
All page backgrounds now pure black (#000000): base token/body/manifest/native + page roots ink-12->ink-13; cards stay ink-10 for contrast
Fix e2e: device-card check matched the raw hex class [class*=262626] which the token sweep removed; now matches the ink-10 token class
Bottom nav: align to bottom edge on iOS (drop safe-area-inset reservation; fixed 8px gap) so it no longer floats above the Home indicator

Radius unification: migrate all legacy rounded-md/lg/xl to canonical scale (md->m 8px, lg/xl->l 12px) so every surface uses s/m/l/pill; cards & dialogs now uniform 12px, inputs/tiles consistent (modal sheet tops keep their 28px arbitrary radius)
Color tokens: snap off-palette grays to ink scale (#A0A0A5/#AEAEB2->ink-6, #636366->ink-7 per config intent) and unify #FF3530->danger; removes off-palette colors
Design-token sweep (components): same value-identical hex->token + rounded-sm->rounded-m pass over src/components

Design-token sweep (pages): replace value-identical hardcoded hex (#262626->ink-10, #BFBFBF->ink-6, #01D6BE->primary, etc.) with tokens; rounded-sm->rounded-m (both 8px). Zero visual change, consistency only
UI polish: enlarge Overview sheet close buttons 28->36px (tap target) + scale-on-press; replace transition-all on shared Button and BottomNavigation with specific properties

Fix asymmetric card corners: rounded-l(12px)/rounded-s(4px) tokens collided with Tailwind built-in side utilities (rounded-l=left, rounded-s=start), making left corners 4px and right 12px; force all-corners in index.css
Bottom nav: position adapts to device safe-area (calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom)+12px)); fixes inconsistent floating gap on devices without a safe-area inset (was conflicting pb-6 + 34px fallback)

WiFi setup: auto-scan AP list on entering Select Wi-Fi (no more manual Scan Again); retry once when first scan returns empty (device scan latency); stay on list to pick SSID (was wrongly jumping to password); dedupe/empty-SSID filter
Fix BLE add-device "serial number cannot be empty": provisioning now sends deviceSerialNumber=DTUID + isVirtualSerialNumber (no separate inverter SN available during BLE setup)
Fix: BLE-added device not appearing in list — add-device sent wrong payload (duid/name); now sends deviceName+dtuDtuid+stationId (reuses a station or creates one) and surfaces real bind failures
Remove dead USB/Web-Serial code: delete serialModbus.ts, useProtocol.ts, useConnectionStatus.tsx; strip serial from connectionStore + ProtocolType
Add Device: remove Connect via USB (RS485) option; WiFi provisioning auto-retries a command once after reconnecting if GATT drops mid-write

Fix WiFi setup GATT disconnect: reconnect-on-demand before each BLE provisioning command (Web + native) so idle GATT drops auto-recover; native scan falls back to service-UUID filter when SSL_ prefix misses; friendlier disconnect error copy

WiFi setup (BLE provisioning) now works on native iOS/Android: bleProvision.ts refactored to dual backend (Web Bluetooth + @capacitor-community/bluetooth-le) behind a shared base class; same FEE7/FED5/FED6 protocol, AES+Base64+packet framing, CID command set per spec v2.9
Android: CI builds a downloadable debug APK and publishes it as a GitHub Release asset (apk-v<version>)
Native push: APNs/FCM token registration listeners (registration/received/action) report token to backend (nativePush.ts); wired on login + push-toggle, torn down on logout

Native BLE: bleManager now has dual backend - Web Bluetooth (PWA) + @capacitor-community/bluetooth-le (native), selected by isNativePlatform; same GATT UUIDs/callbacks (native transfer layer needs real-device validation)

Native (Capacitor) permissions: permissions.ts + notifications now use native plugins on iOS/Android (push/local-notifications, camera, bluetooth-le) via isNativePlatform branch; Android manifest permissions added; iOS Info.plist + setup documented in docs/NATIVE_SETUP.md

iOS/Android adaptation: ship real PNG icons (192/512/apple-touch-180/badge-96) — iOS ignores SVG home-screen icons and Android notification icons must be raster; manifest now PNG + maskable; apple-touch-icon PNG + apple-mobile-web-app-title; notifications use existing PNG icon/badge; outage notif goes through SW (iOS PWA compatible)

Fix Real-Time Power chart: history never loaded (useHistoryFetcher checked res.success which does not exist -> now isApiSuccess(res.code)); widen zoom/pan gesture area to whole chart + add desktop wheel-zoom

Web Push (client side): subscribe via VAPID + report PushSubscription to backend on enabling notifications; idempotent re-sync on launch; unsubscribe on logout. Workbox SW now importScripts sw-push.js so server pushes display when app is closed/locked (requires backend VAPID push to deliver)

Low battery: banner threshold text now reflects Settings value; global low-battery monitor polls all devices app-wide and pushes a system notification when SOC crosses the threshold (fires while app runs in foreground or background; 30-min per-device throttle)
Insights: remove Battery Health card (ring + Battery/Temperature/Days/Health tiles)
Unify battery remaining/to-full time across app: DebugParamsPage + DevicePage low-battery banner now use batteryTimeLabel (capacity=acInvOutputPowerx2 Wh); removed duplicate/old buggy formulas
Battery ring time estimate: default rated capacity fallback 5000Wh -> 1000Wh when nameplate not cached
Fix battery ring time estimate: use real capacity (acInvOutputPower x2 Wh) so remaining/to-full = energyWh / power (was treating SOC%% as Wh, e.g. wrong 11m)

UI polish: tabular numbers on live values (no jitter), scale-on-press feedback on buttons, balanced heading/body text wrap, skip page-enter animation on first load, replace transition-all with explicit properties
Remove swipe-to-back gesture on Overview and Device Monitor pages (prevent accidental navigation)

Real-Time Power chart: real time X-axis (12am/4am/8am/12pm/4pm/8pm/12am), pinch-to-zoom + pan (min 1 hour), today's API history (paginated + IndexedDB cache); realtime value shown only in top-right badge
Standardize backend API params (isApiSuccess, normalizeCountryCode, CaptchaIntent) + document page/label/API conventions in CLAUDE.md
Unify UI metric labels: battery=Battery(%), AC input=AC, temperature=Temperature(°F); remove dead pages (DeviceInfoPage, PeakShavingPage)
Device list: fetch every device's battery on enter + refresh all every 60s (was only updating one device due to single-slot state)
Device monitor: back arrow + swipe now return to Device list (was going back in history)
Unified page transition animations across all routes (no flicker switching Overview ↔ Device list)
Overview: horizontal swipe gesture returns to Device list page
Fix: username update 'illegal argument' (no userId sent to iotUserInfo)
Fix: password update 'illegal argument' (no userId sent to authPassword)
EmailJS configured for in-app feedback form
